title: Phloeophila cryptantha
taxonomy: Phloeophila cryptantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Epidendroideae, which comprises the majority of orchids. It is further classified under the tribe Epidendreae. The genus Phloeophila is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is characterized by small to medium-sized orchids with delicate flowers. Phloeophila cryptantha is native to Central and South America, where it grows as an epiphyte in humid forests. Its classification reflects its close relationship to other orchids within the Epidendroideae subfamily.

intro: Phloeophila cryptantha, also known as Cryptanthus, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to South America, characterized by its unique, flat leaves and small, white flowers. Traditionally, Phloeophila cryptantha has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phloeophila cryptantha is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly prepared by drying and grinding the plant's leaves and flowers.
